title: Urgent Invoice
scenario: A supplier emails an urgent invoice and instructs you to pay immediately via a new bank account. The email claims the request is time-sensitive and will be processed today only if you act now.

answers.B: Use the new bank account details as provided.
answers.C: Verify the invoice and bank details using a known contact method.
answers.D: Escalate to finance and confirm via the company’s official payment process.

explanation: Payment-pressure scams often redirect payments to fraudulent accounts. Confirm changes through established internal processes and known contacts.
proTip: When bank details change, verify through a trusted workflow—not the email sender.
